headline: Does General Mills' (GIS) Supply Chain Shift and Cleaner Labels Recast Its Margin Narrative?
summary: In early March 2026, General Mills named long-time executive Jonathan Ness as Chief Supply Chain Officer and confirmed that its entire K–12 school foods portfolio is now made without certified colors, ahead of its previously stated summer 2026 goal. This combination of leadership continuity in supply chain and faster execution on cleaner-label offerings could be important for how investors assess General Mills’ operational resilience and product positioning. We’ll now examine how Ness’s...

headline: Countdown to General Mills (GIS) Q3 Earnings: A Look at Estimates Beyond Revenue and EPS
summary: Evaluate the expected performance of General Mills (GIS) for the quarter ended February 2026, looking beyond the conventional Wall Street top-and-bottom-line estimates and examining some of its key metrics for better insight.

headline: Campbell’s posts one of worst quarters in years: revenues fell 5%, organic sales dropped 3%
summary: One market analyst put it bluntly on air this week: “Yesterday, Campbell’s reported one of the worst quarters I’ve seen in ages. It was awful. Across the board, revenues fell 5%, organic sales dropped 3%.” That’s not hyperbole. The numbers back it up. Campbell’s (NASDAQ:CPB) reported its Q2 FY2026 results on March 11, 2026, and ... Campbell’s posts one of worst quarters in years: revenues fell 5%, organic sales dropped 3%
